commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=8d256c71fe66f10180e71cb5d096117358f6e2c8)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Linux systemd gateway scope resolution, user-install handoff, system-unit stop/restart cleanup, and regression tests for stale user-scope versus system-scope unit conflicts.

PR surface: Source +316, Tests +1019. Total +1335 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main and v2026.6.6 still return the user-scope unit when same-name user and system unit files both exist, and the linked issue provides a concrete Linux/WSL restart-cascade report. I did not run live systemd mutation in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action is needed for both the custom-unit compatibility fix and real behavior proof, so this should stay on maintainer/author review rather than a ClawSweeper repair lane.

target.title: Fix systemd gateway unit scope conflicts
target.body:
## Summary

- Closes #79375.
- Fixes systemd gateway handoff when user-scope and system-scope gateway units can both exist after upgrade or scope changes.
- Keeps the resolver from treating a stale canonical user unit as authoritative when a live marker-owned custom system unit is still active or enabled.
- Makes install and lifecycle rollback safer: newly staged user artifacts are removed if system-unit retirement fails, retired system units are restored after activation failures, and conflicting user units are retired before system-unit restart.
- Intentionally does not change service names, generated unit contents beyond the handoff path, provider setup, gateway runtime behavior, or non-systemd platforms.

Reviewers should focus on the systemd scope selection and rollback order: active/enabled conflict choice, custom marker-owned system units such as `openclaw.service`, failure recovery when activation or cleanup fails, and root vs non-root lifecycle behavior.

- Fix classification: root-cause lifecycle/state resolver fix.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: high for review; local proof and regression coverage are current, with live root-level mutation intentionally out of scope.
- Root cause: the gateway systemd resolver used file presence and canonical unit names too early, so stale user-scope units could win over live system-scope state, and the install/restart lifecycle did not preserve active/enabled state before retiring the opposite-scope unit.
- Why this is root-cause fix: the source-of-truth for handoff is the live systemd unit state plus the marker-owned unit identity; the patch makes resolver, install, restart, and rollback all consume that same lifecycle state instead of masking port conflicts after the fact.
- Why it matters / User impact: users upgrading or moving between user-managed and system-managed gateway units can otherwise get dueling services, gateway crash loops, or dropped messages when two units fight over the same service/port.
- What did NOT change: this PR only changes Linux systemd gateway service handoff and recovery; it does not change service names, public config schema, provider routing, gateway protocol, non-systemd platforms, or unrelated startup behavior.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: canonical boundary is `src/daemon/systemd.ts`; live systemd active/enabled state and marker-owned unit discovery are the source-of-truth for deciding which scope owns the gateway, while tests cover the raw unit-file and command-contract boundary.
- Target test file: `src/daemon/systemd.test.ts`, covering the resolver, install handoff, rollback, and service control paths touched by `src/daemon/systemd.ts`.
- Scenario locked in: stale canonical user unit vs live marker-owned system unit, inactive/disabled system units, failed system retirement, failed user activation cleanup, root system restart failure, and non-root sudo guidance.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: these regression tests exercise the systemctl call order and filesystem unit artifacts at the lifecycle boundary where the bug occurs; broader live host mutation is higher risk and less deterministic for contributor proof.
- Patch quality notes: patch-quality warnings are expected from test mock defaults and explicit catch/default branches around unavailable service discovery; the production broad catches only downgrade optional discovery or cleanup failures so they do not hide the primary systemd action result.
- Risk labels considered: availability, compatibility, automation, security-boundary, and session-state.
- Risk explanation: the highest-risk area is systemd unit lifecycle ordering because the code may stop/disable one unit before starting another during upgrade recovery.
- Why acceptable: the patch records active/enabled state before retirement, restores retired units on activation or restart failure, does not modify user units before the non-root sudo guard, and reports cleanup failures without turning a successful system action into a false failure.

## Linked context

Closes #79375.

Related review context:
- The PR previously still had `status: ⏳ waiting on author` even after proof labels were present.
- The latest update addresses the reviewer/bot concern that marker-owned custom system units could be misclassified or left competing with canonical user units.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: upgrade/scope-change systems can end up with both user-scope and system-scope OpenClaw gateway units, causing dueling services, crash loops, or message loss.
- Real environment tested: Linux OpenClaw checkout on a systemd host, using a read-only gateway status scan of the installed service state plus regression coverage of the service handoff branches.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```bash
  corepack pnpm@11.2.2 openclaw gateway status --no-probe --deep --json
  corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . exec vitest run src/daemon/systemd.test.ts
  ```

- Evidence after fix (copied terminal output, redacted to omit machine paths and environment values):

  ```json
  {
    "service": {
      "label": "systemd user",
      "loaded": true,
      "loadedText": "enabled",
      "runtime": {
        "status": "stopped",
        "state": "inactive",
        "subState": "dead",
        "systemd": {
          "unit": "openclaw-gateway.service",
          "killMode": "control-group"
        }
      }
    },
    "gateway": {
      "bindMode": "loopback",
      "bindHost": "127.0.0.1",
      "port": 18789,
      "portSource": "service args"
    },
    "port": {
      "port": 18789,
      "status": "free",
      "listeners": []
    },
    "extraServices": []
  }
  ```

- Supplemental regression output:

  ```text
  corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . exec vitest run src/daemon/systemd.test.ts

  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  104 passed (104)

  corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . exec vitest run src/daemon/systemd.test.ts -t "systemd service control"

  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  12 passed | 92 skipped (104)
  ```

- Observed result after fix: the live read-only status command sees a single installed systemd user gateway unit, no extra discovered services, and no listener occupying the configured gateway port; the patched resolver/control coverage then verifies that stale opposite-scope units are retired or restored in the correct order when those conflict states are present.
- What was not tested: no live root-level system unit was created, stopped, disabled, or restarted on a host machine during this update.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: destructive host-level systemd mutation was intentionally kept out of this contributor validation; the live proof is a read-only status scan, while the conflict/rollback branches use filesystem-backed unit artifacts and deterministic `systemctl` responses to exercise root/non-root, active/enabled, disabled/inactive, and cleanup-failure states.
- Before evidence: the added regression tests fail on the previous behavior when marker-owned custom system units are ignored, replacement user cleanup disables the wrong unit, cleanup failure skips system-unit restore, or system restart runs before retiring an active user-scope conflict.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

```bash
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . exec vitest run src/daemon/systemd.test.ts -t "restores the custom system unit"
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . exec vitest run src/daemon/systemd.test.ts -t "systemd service control"
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . exec vitest run src/daemon/systemd.test.ts
```

Regression coverage added or updated:
- Active system unit wins over stale user unit when both scopes exist.
- Inactive/disabled system unit does not displace the user unit.
- Marker-owned custom system units participate in resolver and install retirement decisions.
- User install rolls back newly staged user unit/env files when system-unit retirement fails.
- User activation rollback disables the canonical replacement user unit, not the retired custom system unit name.
- User activation rollback restores the retired system unit even if user cleanup fails.
- System-scope restart retires a conflicting user unit before restart and restores that user unit if restart fails.
- Non-root control still stops before modifying user units and asks for sudo for system-scope control.

What failed before this fix:
- The new regression cases reproduced the stale-user/custom-system conflict ordering and rollback gaps before the implementation changes.

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes` — systemd install/restart now actively resolves live opposite-scope gateway conflicts instead of leaving dueling units in place.
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `Yes` — only for systemd gateway unit handoff/rollback behavior during install and service control.
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`.
- Highest-risk area: systemd unit lifecycle ordering during upgrade or scope migration.
- Risk mitigation: preserve live active/enabled state before retiring units, restore retired units on activation/restart failure, avoid user-unit cleanup before non-root sudo guard, and keep cleanup failures from hiding the primary systemd action result.

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer/bot review after the updated branch and PR body are submitted.

Waiting-on-author items addressed:
- RF-001 `status: ⏳ waiting on author`: this update adds the missing custom marker-owned system unit handling and rollback/restart ordering coverage, then refreshes the PR body for maintainer review.
- RF-002 real behavior proof signal: the PR body now includes copied after-fix live status output plus supplemental regression output, and describes the systemd-specific proof limitations directly.
- ClawSweeper advisory: custom marker-owned system units are now included in installed-scope resolution and user-install conflict retirement.
- Independent diff review findings addressed: user activation rollback disables the replacement user unit, restores the retired system unit even if user cleanup fails, and retires a conflicting user unit before system restart.

Still waiting on: remote CI and maintainer review after this update is submitted.
